info.name: Klupni MVP
info.description:
API collection for Klupni MVP backend. Base URL and access token are managed via collection variables.

All responses use the envelope format: `{ success, code, message, data, meta }`. Payload is in `data`; paginated responses include `meta`.

Variables:
- `baseUrl`: http://localhost:3000
- `accessToken`: populated automatically after login
- `activityId`: populated automatically after creating an activity
- `participationId`: populated automatically after getting activity details
- `externalContactId`: populated automatically after creating an external contact
- `contactId`: set manually from list contacts response
- `invitationId`: populated automatically after sending an invitation

request.description:
Register a new user. Creates the user, an empty user_profile, and sends a verification email.

Responds 201 with `{ id, email, message }`.

query.description: Token received in the verification email

request.description:
Verify the user's email using the token received by email. The token is valid for 24 hours and can only be used once.

Responds 200 with `{ message }`.

Resend the verification email. **Public** endpoint.

Request: `{ email: string }`

- If the email is not registered: responds 200 with generic message (no email enumeration)
- If the email is already verified: responds 400
- Otherwise: invalidates previous unused tokens, creates new one, sends email, responds 200

Responds 200 with `{ message }`.

Request a password reset email. **Public** endpoint.

Request: `{ email: string }`

- Always responds 200 with generic message (no email enumeration)
- Only sends email if account exists and is verified
- Token expires in 1 hour
- Link points to FRONTEND_URL/reset-password?token=xxx

Reset password using the token from the email. **Public** endpoint.

Request: `{ token: string, password: string }` (password min 8 chars)

Responds 200 with `{ message: 'Password has been reset successfully' }`.
Responds 400 if token is invalid, expired or already used.

Login with email and password. The email must be verified first.

Responds 200 with `{ accessToken, user }` and sets an HttpOnly cookie `refresh_token`.

The test script automatically saves `accessToken` to the collection variable.

Generate a new access token using the `refresh_token` HttpOnly cookie. The cookie must be present (set automatically by Postman after login if cookie handling is enabled).

Responds 200 with `{ accessToken }`.

The test script automatically saves the new `accessToken` to the collection variable.

Logout the authenticated user. Clears the `refresh_token` cookie.

Requires a valid `Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>` header.

Responds 200 with `{ message }`.

query.description: Username to check (3-30 chars, alphanumeric + underscore)
Optional: exclude this user ID (e.g. when updating own profile)

request.description:
Check if a username is available. **Public** endpoint.

Query params:
- `username` (required): 3-30 chars, letters, numbers, underscores only
- `excludeUserId` (optional): when updating profile, pass current user ID to exclude own username

Responds 200 with envelope. `data.available` (boolean) indicates availability. Code: `USERNAME_CHECK_RESULT`.
Responds 400 if username format is invalid.

query.description: Search by email or username (min 2 chars)

request.description:
Search users by email or username. For inviting users to activities. Requires auth. Paginated.

**Query params:**
- `q` (required): search term, min 2 characters. Matches email or username (partial, case-insensitive).
- `page` (optional): page number, default 1.
- `limit` (optional): items per page, default 10, max 100.

Responds 200 with `{ data: [{ id, email, firstName, lastName, username }], meta: { total, page, limit, totalPages } }`.
Excludes unverified users and the current user.

Get the authenticated user's account information (without profile details).

Responds 200 with `{ id, email, emailVerifiedAt, createdAt, profileId }`.

Get the authenticated user's profile details.

Responds 200 with `{ id, userId, firstName, lastName, username, avatarUrl, createdAt, updatedAt }`.

Update the authenticated user's profile (used during onboarding or later).

All fields are optional:
- `firstName` (string, 1-50 chars)
- `lastName` (string, 1-50 chars)
- `username` (string, 3-30 chars, alphanumeric + underscores, must be unique)
- `avatarUrl` (valid URL, max 500 chars)

If `username` is taken, responds 409.

Responds 200 with the updated profile `{ id, userId, firstName, lastName, username, avatarUrl, createdAt, updatedAt }`.

Get the public profile of any user by their user ID.

Responds 200 with `{ id, userId, firstName, lastName, username, avatarUrl }`.

Does not include timestamps (unlike your own profile endpoint).

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Fútbol 5 el sábado",
  "description": "Partido amistoso en la cancha del barrio",
  "startAt": "2026-03-15T18:00:00.000Z",
  "endAt": "2026-03-15T20:00:00.000Z",
  "sportName": "Fútbol",
  "locationText": "Cancha Municipal, Av. Principal 123",
  "maxParticipants": 10,
  "minParticipants": 6
}

request.description:
Create a new activity. The authenticated user becomes the host automatically (role: 'host' in activity_participations).

The activity is created together with its `activity_open` record in a single transaction.

**Validations:**
- `title`: required, max 100 chars
- `startAt`: required, must be a future date (ISO 8601)
- `endAt`: optional, must be after `startAt`
- `maxParticipants`: required, 2–100
- `minParticipants`: required, min 1, must be ≤ `maxParticipants`

Responds 201 with the full activity including `activityOpen`.

The test script automatically saves `activityId` to the collection variable.

List activities relevant to the authenticated user.

**Query params:**
- `type`: 'all' (default) | 'created' | 'participating'
- `time`: 'upcoming' (default) | 'past' | 'all'

By default only upcoming activities are shown (startAt >= now), ordered by nearest first.
With `time=past`, shows past activities ordered by most recent first.
With `time=all`, shows all activities ordered by startAt ASC.

Each item includes a `participantCount` with the number of active participants.

Responds 200 with an array.

Get full detail of a single activity by ID.

Includes:
- `activityOpen` data (sport, location, min/max participants)
- `participants` list with role, joinedAt, and user info (email) or external contact info

Responds 404 if the activity does not exist or is soft-deleted.

The test script saves the first non-host `participationId` to the collection variable.

List participants of an activity from activity_participations only. Pending invitations are listed in GET /activities/:activityId/invitations.

**Query params:**
- `status=all` (default): all participants
- `status=confirmed`: only participants with confirmed_at set
- `status=unconfirmed`: only participants with confirmed_at null

Each item includes: `type` (user/external_contact/free), `participationId`, `role`, `status` (active | removed | left), `confirmedAt` (ISO date when confirmed, null if not), `hasPendingInvitation`, `displayName`, `avatarUrl`, `userId`, `externalContactId`.

Use `userId` or `externalContactId` to fetch detailed profiles via the dedicated endpoints.

Update an existing activity. Only the host can do this (403 otherwise).

All fields are optional. Only the fields sent will be updated. The same validations from creation apply.

Responds 200 with the updated activity including `activityOpen`.

Soft-delete an activity (sets `deleted_at` on both `activities` and `activity_open`). Only the host can do this (403 otherwise).

Responds 200 with `{ message: 'Activity deleted successfully' }`.

Add a participant (user or external contact) without sending an invitation. Participant remains unconfirmed (confirmed_at null) until they confirm.

Exactly one of userId or externalContactId must be provided.
- userId: registered app user (cannot add yourself)
- externalContactId: must belong to you

Responds 201 with participation data including confirmedAt: null.

Add a free participant to the activity. Host-only.

A free participant has no linked user account or external contact — they only exist within this activity.

Required fields:
- `alias` (string, 1-100 chars): a display name to identify the participant

Free participants can only have the role `participant` and cannot be made host.

Checks:
- Caller must be host
- Activity must not exceed `maxParticipants`

Responds 201 with `{ id, activityId, alias, role, status, joinedAt, createdAt }`.

Change the role of an active participant. Only a host can do this.

- Cannot change your own role (400)
- External contacts cannot be assigned the `host` role (400)
- Multiple hosts are allowed
- Valid values: `'host'` or `'participant'`

Responds 200 with the updated participation.

Remove an active participant from the activity. Only a host can do this.

- Cannot remove another host (400 — demote them to participant first)
- Sets `status = 'removed'` on the participation row (no hard delete)

Responds 200 with `{ message: 'Participant removed successfully' }`.

Leave an activity as the authenticated user.

- If the user is not an active participant: 404
- If the user is the **only** host: 400 — must assign another host first
- Sets `status = 'left'` on the participation row

Responds 200 with `{ message: 'You have left the activity' }`.

Add a contact to the unified list. Exactly one of `userId` or `externalContactId` must be provided.

- `userId`: registered app user (cannot add yourself)
- `externalContactId`: must belong to you (owner_user_id)

Responds 201 with envelope. Code: `CONTACT_ADDED`.

List unified contacts (app users + external contacts). Paginated.

Query params:
- `type`: 'user' | 'external_contact' | 'all' (default: all)
- `page`, `limit`

Responds 200 with envelope. Code: `CONTACT_LIST_RETRIEVED`.

Remove multiple contacts from the list (soft delete). Requires auth.

Body: `{ ids: string[] }` (1-50 UUIDs). Only contacts that belong to you are deleted; others are ignored.

Responds 200 with envelope. Code: `CONTACT_BATCH_REMOVED`. Data: `{ deletedCount: number }`.

Remove a contact from the list (soft delete). Requires auth.

Responds 200 with envelope. Code: `CONTACT_REMOVED`.
Responds 404 if contact not found.

List all external contacts owned by the authenticated user. Paginated.

Responds 200 with `{ data: [...], meta: { total, page, limit, totalPages } }`.

Excludes soft-deleted contacts.

Create a standalone external contact (not tied to any activity). Automatically adds it to the unified contacts list.

Required fields:
- `alias` (string, 1-100 chars)

Optional fields:
- `email` (valid email)
- `phoneNumber` (string, max 30 chars)

Responds 201 with envelope. `data` contains `{ id, alias, email, phoneNumber, createdAt }`.

The test script saves `externalContactId` to the collection variable for use in invitation endpoints.

Update an external contact that belongs to the authenticated user.

All fields are optional:
- `alias` (string, 1-100 chars)
- `email` (valid email)
- `phoneNumber` (string, max 30 chars)

Responds 200 with `{ id, alias, email, phoneNumber, createdAt, updatedAt }`.
Responds 404 if the contact does not exist or does not belong to you.

Get detailed information of a specific external contact.

Responds 200 with `{ id, alias, email, phoneNumber, createdAt, updatedAt }`.

Responds 404 if the contact does not exist.

Soft-delete an external contact. Only the owner can delete it.

Responds 200 with `{ message: 'External contact deleted successfully' }`.
Responds 404 if the contact does not exist or does not belong to you.

Invite a registered user or an external contact to the activity. Only the host can send invitations.

Exactly **one** of the following must be provided:
- `userId`: UUID of a registered Klupni user
- `externalContactId`: UUID of an external contact owned by the host (must have an email)

**Checks performed:**
- Activity must exist, not be deleted, and have status `open`
- Active participants + pending invitations must be under `maxParticipants`
- No duplicate pending invitation for the same target
- The target must not already be an active participant
- Cannot invite yourself
- External contact must belong to the host and have an email

Sends an invitation email with an accept link. Token expires in 48 hours.

Responds 201 with `{ id, email, userId, externalContactId, status, expiresAt }`.

The test script saves `invitationId` to the collection variable.

Invite multiple users and/or external contacts at once. Only the host can send invitations.

**Request body:**
- `userIds` (optional): array of user UUIDs (max 50)
- `externalContactIds` (optional): array of external contact UUIDs (max 50)
- At least one array must be non-empty. Total recipients ≤ 50.

**Response:** `{ created: [{ id, email, userId?, externalContactId?, status, expiresAt }], failed: [{ userId?, externalContactId?, reason }], message }`

Invalid targets (already participating, pending invite, no email, etc.) are skipped and listed in `failed`.

query.description: Filter: pending | accepted | cancelled | all (default: all)

request.description:
List invitations for an activity. Only the host can view them. Paginated.

**Query params:**
- `status=all` (default): all invitations
- `status=pending`: only pending invitations
- `status=accepted`: only accepted invitations
- `status=cancelled`: only cancelled invitations

Responds 200 with `{ data: [...], meta: { total, page, limit, totalPages } }`.

query.description: Filter: pending | accepted | past | expired | all (default: all)

request.description:
List invitations received by the authenticated user. Paginated.

**Query params:**
- `status=all` (default): all invitations
- `status=pending`: only pending invitations
- `status=accepted`: only accepted invitations
- `status=past`: invitations for activities that already happened (history)
- `status=expired`: only expired invitations

Responds 200 with `{ data: [...], meta: { total, page, limit, totalPages } }`.
Each item includes activity details and inviter info.

query.description: Invitation token from the email link.

request.description:
Preview invitation details without accepting. **Public** endpoint (no auth required).

Use before showing the accept page: display activity title, date, location, inviter name, and whether the invitation can still be accepted.

Responds 200 with:
- `activity`: { id, title, startAt, endAt, locationText, sportName, status }
- `inviter`: { displayName, email }
- `status`: 'pending' | 'accepted' | 'rejected' | 'cancelled' | 'expired'
- `expiresAt`: ISO date
- `canAccept`: boolean (true only if pending, not expired, activity open)

Responds 400 if token is invalid.

query.description: Invitation token from the email link. Copy from Mailtrap and paste in the invitationToken collection variable.

request.description:
Accept an invitation using the token from the email link. This is a **public** endpoint (no auth required).

**Checks performed:**
- Token must exist and be in `pending` status
- Token must not be expired (auto-marks as `expired` if it is)
- Activity must still be `open` with available spots

On success:
- Creates a participation (role: `participant`, status: `active`)
- Updates invitation to `accepted` with `respondedAt`
- Sends a notification email to the host

Responds 200 with `{ message, activity: { id, title, startAt, locationText } }`.

Cancel a pending invitation. Only the host can do this.

- The invitation must be in `pending` status (400 otherwise)
- Sets `status = 'cancelled'`

Responds 200 with `{ message: 'Invitation cancelled' }`.
